content_en:
Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Ibrahim ibn Abi Bakr ibn Khallikan was born in 608 AH (1211 CE) in Irbil (present-day Iraq), into a family that claimed descent from the Barmakid viziers who had served the Abbasid caliphs. From his earliest years he was immersed in the culture of learning that characterized the educated classes of his era. He pursued his education in Mosul and then traveled extensively, studying in Aleppo, Damascus, Cairo, and other centers of Islamic scholarship — a pattern of travel in search of knowledge (rihla) that was the expected path for any serious scholar.

Ibn Khallikan's era was one of dramatic political change but remarkable cultural continuity. The Ayyubid dynasty, established by Saladin, was in its final decades when he was young, giving way to Mamluk rule in Egypt and Syria during his mature years. The Mongol catastrophe struck in 1258, destroying Baghdad and ending the Abbasid caliphate — an event of shattering psychological impact on the Muslim world. Yet the Syrian and Egyptian scholarly centers where Ibn Khallikan worked continued to flourish, supported by Mamluk patronage and a tradition of scholarship that proved remarkably resilient.

Ibn Khallikan rose to the highest levels of the legal establishment, serving as chief Shafi'i judge (qadi al-qudat) in Syria under Mamluk appointment. This position gave him access to the major libraries and scholarly networks of the era, as well as the administrative experience to understand the practical dimensions of Islamic legal institutions. He served two separate terms as chief judge, interrupted by a period when he was displaced through court politics — an experience that gave him time to devote to his biographical work.

The intellectual environment that shaped Ibn Khallikan was one of intense interest in the past. The destruction of Baghdad had disrupted but also intensified Islamic civilization's relationship with its own history. Scholars felt an urgent need to document and preserve the record of Islamic civilization's achievements. Biographical literature — the art of recording the lives of distinguished men — had a long tradition in Arabic, going back to the earliest period of Islamic scholarship, and Ibn Khallikan brought this tradition to a new level of cultural breadth and literary elegance. He died in Damascus in 681 AH (1282 CE).
content_id:
Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Ibrahim ibn Abi Bakr ibn Khallikan lahir pada 608 H (1211 M) di Irbil (Irak modern), dari keluarga yang mengklaim keturunan dari para wazir Barmakid yang melayani para khalifah Abbasiyah. Sejak masa mudanya ia terbenam dalam budaya pembelajaran yang mencirikan kelas-kelas terpelajar di eranya. Ia mengejar pendidikannya di Mosul kemudian melakukan perjalanan luas, belajar di Aleppo, Damaskus, Kairo, dan pusat-pusat keilmuan Islam lainnya — pola perjalanan dalam menuntut ilmu (rihlah) yang merupakan jalur yang diharapkan bagi setiap ulama yang serius.

Era Ibn Khallikan adalah era perubahan politik yang dramatis tetapi kesinambungan budaya yang luar biasa. Dinasti Ayyubiyah, yang didirikan oleh Salahuddin al-Ayyubi, berada di dekade-dekade terakhirnya ketika ia masih muda, memberikan jalan kepada kekuasaan Mamluk di Mesir dan Suriah selama tahun-tahun dewasanya. Bencana Mongol terjadi pada tahun 1258, menghancurkan Baghdad dan mengakhiri kekhalifahan Abbasiyah — sebuah peristiwa dengan dampak psikologis yang menghancurkan dunia Muslim. Namun pusat-pusat keilmuan Suriah dan Mesir tempat Ibn Khallikan bekerja terus berkembang, didukung oleh patronase Mamluk dan tradisi keilmuan yang terbukti sangat tahan banting.

Ibn Khallikan naik ke tingkat tertinggi dari institusi hukum, menjabat sebagai hakim kepala Syafi'i (qadhi al-qudhah) di Suriah atas penunjukan Mamluk. Jabatan ini memberinya akses ke perpustakaan-perpustakaan utama dan jaringan-jaringan keilmuan di era tersebut, serta pengalaman administratif untuk memahami dimensi-dimensi praktis dari institusi-institusi hukum Islam. Ia menjabat dua periode terpisah sebagai hakim kepala, diselingi oleh periode ketika ia dipindahkan melalui politik istana.

Lingkungan intelektual yang membentuk Ibn Khallikan adalah lingkungan dengan minat yang intens terhadap masa lalu. Penghancuran Baghdad telah mengganggu tetapi juga mengintensifkan hubungan peradaban Islam dengan sejarahnya sendiri. Para ulama merasakan kebutuhan mendesak untuk mendokumentasikan dan melestarikan catatan pencapaian peradaban Islam. Literatur biografis memiliki tradisi panjang dalam bahasa Arab, dan Ibn Khallikan membawa tradisi ini ke tingkat keluasan budaya dan keanggunan sastra yang baru. Ia wafat di Damaskus pada 681 H (1282 M).
